channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliamentary Inquiry Examines Dormant Sites and Lease Compliance

item.title: India disputes Pakistan's water scarcity claims, citing treaty data and domestic mismanagement
item.description: The article discusses India's rejection of Pakistan's allegations regarding water scarcity in the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) framework. Indian authorities argue that Pakistan continues to receive highe...

item.title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' Faces Backlash Over Controversial Casting Decisions
item.description: Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's 'The Odyssey' has faced significant backlash due to its casting choices, which critics argue undermine the source material. The film's promotional campaign, p...

item.title: Tanzanian Authorities Restrict Political Gatherings Amid Tensions
item.description: Tanzanian authorities have imposed a ban on public political gatherings in the Prime region, marking a significant restriction on political activities. This decision comes amid heightened political te...

item.title: Super Subbu: A Humorous Take on Sex Education Challenges in Rural India
item.description: The Telugu series 'Super Subbu' on Netflix follows the struggles of Subbu, a sex education instructor tasked with teaching villagers in Maakipur, a conservative village proud of its high fertility rat...

item.title: Deadly Crash on Sea-to-Sky Highway Closes Road for Hours
item.description: A fatal collision on the Sea-to-Sky Highway near the Deeks Creek Bridge, south of Porteau Cove, caused a multi-hour road closure on Sunday. Police confirmed the crash was fatal, though details remain ...

item.title: 10 Fascinating Facts About So Ji-Sub, the Star of 'Agent Kim Reactivated'
item.description: The article explores lesser-known aspects of South Korean actor So Ji-Sub, who is currently starring in the Netflix K-drama 'Agent Kim Reactivated'. It highlights his versatile acting career spanning ...

item.description: The article details the tense courtroom scene involving Tyler Robinson, the murder suspect in the case of Charlie Kirk, and Erika, his alleged victim's widow. During the trial, Robinson was observed l...

item.title: Navigating the Challenges of Italian Trains: A Traveler's Experience

item.title: Senate impeachment court says Sara Duterte cannot be required to personally attend impeachment proceedings
item.description: The spokesperson of the Senate impeachment court said that Vice President Sara Duterte cannot be compelled to personally attend her impeachment trial because she is protected by her constitutional rig...

item.title: Canada Selects German Firm to Build Up to 12 Submarines in Major Defence Procurement
item.description: The Canadian government has finalized a deal with Germany’s ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems (TKMS) to build up to 12 submarines, marking the largest defence procurement in Canadian history. Prime Minister...

item.title: Karnataka CM Shivakumar Denies Govt Interference in SIR, Accuses Opposition of Political Tactics
item.description: Karnataka Chief Minister D K Shivakumar denied allegations of government interference in the ongoing electoral roll verification (SIR) process, accusing the BJP and JD(S) of using the issue for politi...

item.title: Teen Denied Legal Abortion Due to Religious Objections Seeks Unsafe Procedure in Limpopo
item.description: An 18-year-old from rural Limpopo, South Africa, was denied a legal abortion at a public clinic after healthcare workers refused care on religious grounds, forcing her to seek an illegal procedure fro...

item.title: Australian filmmaker uses AI to create groundbreaking wildlife documentary
item.description: Jodie Heenan's AI-generated documentary 'Guardians of the Burrow' won an award at the Omni AI film festival, showcasing how artificial intelligence can capture intricate natural interactions. The film...

item.title: UNICEF reports over 300 children killed or injured in Sudan conflict within six months
item.description: A United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) report has revealed that more than 300 children have been killed or injured in the ongoing conflict in Sudan within a six-month period. The findings, released...
